evals.prompt: Create k6 load test scripts for our REST API. Need 3 scenarios: baseline (50 VUs for 5 min), spike (ramp to 200 VUs over 30s, hold 1 min, ramp down), and soak (30 VUs for 30 min). Endpoints: GET /api/products (list), POST /api/orders (create), GET /api/orders/:id (detail). Include auth token setup and think time.
evals.expected_output: k6 scripts with 3 scenarios, proper executors, auth, think time, and thresholds

assertions.name: three_scenarios
assertions.description: Baseline, spike, and soak scenarios defined

assertions.name: think_time
assertions.description: Sleep between requests to model real user behavior

assertions.name: auth_setup
assertions.description: Auth token obtained in setup() function

assertions.name: thresholds
assertions.description: p95 latency and error rate thresholds defined

assertions.name: proper_executors
assertions.description: Uses ramping-vus or constant-arrival-rate, not shared-iterations

evals.prompt: Our API's p95 latency is 800ms under 100 concurrent users. The target is 200ms. Help identify the bottleneck. The API calls PostgreSQL and an external payment provider.
evals.expected_output: Bottleneck analysis approach: isolate DB vs external, profile queries, check connection pooling

assertions.name: isolation_approach
assertions.description: Suggests isolating DB latency vs external API latency

assertions.name: query_profiling
assertions.description: Recommends EXPLAIN ANALYZE on slow queries

assertions.name: connection_pooling
assertions.description: Checks if connection pooling is configured

assertions.name: external_api_timeout
assertions.description: Reviews timeout settings for payment provider calls

assertions.name: specific_metrics
assertions.description: Reports p50/p95/p99 latency breakdown

evals.prompt: Set up k6 load testing in our CI pipeline. Run a smoke test (10 VUs, 30s) on every PR, and a full load test (100 VUs, 5 min) nightly. Fail the PR if p95 > 500ms or error rate > 1%.
evals.expected_output: CI integration with k6, threshold-based PR blocking, and nightly full test

assertions.name: ci_config
assertions.description: GitHub Actions or equivalent workflow for k6

assertions.name: pr_smoke_test
assertions.description: Lightweight smoke test on PRs with thresholds

assertions.name: nightly_full_test
assertions.description: Scheduled full load test with higher VU count

assertions.name: threshold_enforcement
assertions.description: PR blocked if p95 > 500ms or errors > 1%

assertions.name: results_reporting
assertions.description: Test results reported or uploaded as artifacts

evals.prompt: Create a stress test to find the breaking point of our API. Start at 10 VUs, ramp up by 10 every minute, continue until error rate exceeds 10% or p95 exceeds 2 seconds. Report the max sustainable load.
evals.expected_output: k6 stress test with progressive ramping and breaking point detection

assertions.name: progressive_ramp
assertions.description: VUs increase progressively (10, 20, 30... per minute)

assertions.name: abort_conditions
assertions.description: Test stops when error rate > 10% or p95 > 2s

assertions.name: max_load_reported
assertions.description: Reports the max VU count before breaking point

assertions.name: metrics_per_stage
assertions.description: Latency and error metrics reported per ramp stage

assertions.name: think_time
assertions.description: Think time included for realistic load modeling

evals.prompt: Write a k6 test for a WebSocket-based chat application. Test: connect, send 10 messages, receive echoes, and disconnect. Scale to 500 concurrent connections.
evals.expected_output: k6 WebSocket test with connection lifecycle, message exchange, and concurrent scaling

assertions.name: websocket_connection
assertions.description: Uses k6 ws.connect() for WebSocket testing

assertions.name: message_exchange
assertions.description: Sends messages and validates received responses

assertions.name: concurrent_connections
assertions.description: Scales to 500 concurrent WebSocket connections

assertions.name: connection_lifecycle
assertions.description: Tests connect, exchange, and clean disconnect

assertions.name: metrics_reported
assertions.description: Reports connection time, message latency, and error rate
